questions.q: What is the primary function of carbohydrates in the body?
opts: (A) To provide energy for the body
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are the body's primary source of energy. They are broken down into glucose, which is then used by the body to produce energy.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a macronutrient?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates, proteins, and fats are the three main macronutrients. They provide energy and are essential for growth and repair.

questions.q: What is the recommended daily intake of protein for an athlete?
opts: (A) 0.5-1 gram per kilogram of body weight
(B) 1-2 grams per kilogram of body weight
(C) 2-3 grams per kilogram of body weight
(D) 3-4 grams per kilogram of body weight
questions.explanation: The recommended daily intake of protein for an athlete is 1-2 grams per kilogram of body weight to support muscle growth and repair.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating complex carbohydrates?
opts: (A) They provide a quick source of energy
(B) They can help with weight loss
(C) They can help to slow down the release of glucose into the bloodstream
questions.explanation: Complex carbohydrates, such as whole grains and fruits, can help to slow down the release of glucose into the bloodstream, providing a sustained source of energy.

questions.q: What is the main function of fats in the body?
opts: (A) To provide energy for the body
(D) To store energy for later use
questions.explanation: Fats are an important source of energy and are also stored in the body for later use. They are also essential for the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a functional macronutrient?
questions.explanation: Fiber is a functional macronutrient that can help to slow down the release of glucose into the bloodstream and promote regular bowel movements.

questions.q: What is the recommended daily intake of water for an athlete?
questions.explanation: The recommended daily intake of water for an athlete is 12-15 cups per day to help with hydration and prevent dehydration.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating a balanced diet?
opts: (A) It can help to improve performance
(B) It can help to reduce the risk of injury
(C) It can help to promote overall health and well-being
questions.explanation: Eating a balanced diet can help to improve performance, reduce the risk of injury, and promote overall health and well-being.

questions.q: What is the main function of vitamins in the body?
opts: (A) To provide energy for the body
questions.explanation: Vitamins are essential for a variety of bodily functions, including the regulation of metabolism, the maintenance of healthy skin and hair, and the support of immune function.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a mineral?
questions.explanation: Calcium is a mineral that is essential for bone health and development.

questions.q: What is the recommended daily intake of iron for an athlete?
questions.explanation: The recommended daily intake of iron for an athlete is 12-15 milligrams per day to support the production of red blood cells.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating a variety of fruits and vegetables?
opts: (A) They can help to provide a quick source of energy
(B) They can help to support immune function
(C) They can help to promote overall health and well-being
questions.explanation: Eating a variety of fruits and vegetables can help to provide a quick source of energy, support immune function, and promote overall health and well-being.

questions.q: What is the main function of antioxidants in the body?
opts: (A) To provide energy for the body
questions.explanation: Antioxidants, such as vitamins C and E, can help to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a phytochemical?
questions.explanation: Carotenoids are a type of phytochemical that can help to protect cells from damage and support immune function.

questions.q: What is the recommended daily intake of fiber for an athlete?
questions.explanation: The recommended daily intake of fiber for an athlete is 12-15 grams per day to support regular bowel movements and promote overall health.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating a Mediterranean-style diet?
opts: (A) It can help to improve performance
(B) It can help to reduce the risk of injury
(C) It can help to promote overall health and well-being
questions.explanation: Eating a Mediterranean-style diet can help to improve performance, reduce the risk of injury, and promote overall health and well-being.

questions.q: What is the main function of omega-3 fatty acids in the body?
opts: (A) To provide energy for the body
questions.explanation: Omega-3 fatty acids, such as EPA and DHA, can help to support heart health and reduce inflammation.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating a variety of whole grains?
opts: (A) They can help to provide a quick source of energy
(B) They can help to support immune function
(C) They can help to promote overall health and well-being
questions.explanation: Eating a variety of whole grains can help to provide a quick source of energy, support immune function, and promote overall health and well-being.

questions.q: What is the recommended daily intake of potassium for an athlete?
questions.explanation: The recommended daily intake of potassium for an athlete is 4-5 milligrams per day to support muscle function and heart health.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of eating a variety of nuts and seeds?
opts: (A) They can help to provide a quick source of energy
(B) They can help to support immune function
(C) They can help to promote overall health and well-being
questions.explanation: Eating a variety of nuts and seeds can help to provide a quick source of energy, support immune function, and promote overall health and well-being.
